Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand User Input: The user provides a complete prompt intended for an LLM. The prompt contains: A DECLARE model represented as a Python dictionary with various constraint types... Activity descriptions... A detailed request asking for a step-by-step description...

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the DECLARE Model Constraints: I need to carefully parse each constraint type and its meaning in DECLARE notation, then map it to the activities to reconstruct the process flow. DECLARE Constraint Definitions (standard): existence(A): A must occur at least once... Lets map the constraints from the prompt: existence: Receive_Application, Gather_Additional_Documents...

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Given the contradictions/oddities in the synthetic model (noncoexistence between Receive and Transfer, chainprecedence direction), I will construct a coherent, practical narrative that aligns with the intent of the prompt, while accurately referencing the constraints.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the main flow is: Receive_Application -> Preliminary_Credit_Check -> Gather_Additional_Documents <-> Quality_Assurance_Review (alternating) -> Assemble_Loan_Offer_Package -> Transfer_Funds.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Structure the Response: Introduction: Acknowledge the model... Step-by-Step Process Flow... Constraint Analysis & Compliance... Real-World Motivations... Consequences of Violations... Conclusion...

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Draft - Section by Section (Mental Refinement): (Introduction) The DECLARE model provided outlines... (Step-by-Step Flow) 1. Initiation & Intake... 2. Initial Risk Assessment...

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self-Correction/Verification during drafting: Check constraint directions vs. standard DECLARE... noncoexistence(Transfer, Receive): I noted it as a phase separator... The prompt asks to explain step-by-step... discuss how each constraint ensures... My draft covers all these.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait, lets verify chainprecedence direction in the prompt... Thats definitely backwards... I will explicitly state: Note: In formal DECLARE notation, some constraints like chainprecedence(Authorize, Preliminary) may appear directionally inverted... Also, noncoexistence(Transfer, Receive)... This handles the synthetic models quirks gracefully...

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Consequences of Violations: Skipping Preliminary Credit Check: Could lead to... Authorizing Before QA: Exposes the institution to... Transferring Funds Before Assembly/Authorization: A critical failure...
